literal: to remain or continue to be present in a person's mind repeatedly or persistently
used to indicate that something is improbable or unlikely to happen
likely to die soon; near death
to appear to be no older than a stated age; to look at most a certain age
very little; almost nothing in quantity, activity, or occurrence
having prior experience with a situation; not inexperienced
an emphatic refusal; absolutely not
an emphatic and jocular refusal; absolutely not
to leave someone far behind, especially by moving faster so that they cannot catch up
an interjection used to tell someone to wait, slow down, or stop what they are doing
used to say that a situation is unpleasant or regrettable but not disastrous or irreparable
used to introduce an additional point that reinforces or amplifies what has just been said; 'and also'
prefatory phrase used to introduce a blunt, candid, or unvarnished statement; meaning 'to state plainly' or 'to be blunt'
refuse to have any involvement with or approach something or someone because it is considered objectionable, risky, or undesirable
decline to engage with, get involved in, or handle someone or something because it is considered dangerous, unpleasant, or undesirable
be unable to achieve success because of a persistent run of bad luck or repeated setbacks
have no value or importance; be utterly worthless or insignificant
of no monetary or practical value; completely worthless
worthless; having no useful or monetary value
completely worthless; of negligible monetary or practical value
